templates.description: Default complete connection-layer template with Nexus entry, current snapshot, knowledge, tasks, workspaces, evidence chains, logs, brain regions, skills, resources, and responsible-use text.

guide.description: Agent-facing inspection command that writes nothing, prints the minimal interview and backup gate, and recommends start or integrate after detecting existing knowledge files.

start.description: Clean start for a new vault, repository, project, or workspace. Requires an interview-confirmed language before writing.
writes: <target>/<localized Nexus path, e.g. 中枢.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Knowledge path, e.g. 知识/知识.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Task path, e.g. 任务/任务.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Workspace path, e.g. 工作区/工作区.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Evidence Chain path, e.g. 证据链/证据链.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Resource path, e.g. 资源/资源.md for zh-cn>

integrate.description: Non-destructive integration for an existing knowledge base. Writes hidden review records, a native top-layer proposal, and an AGENTS.md patch proposal under .nexubrain/integration/. The active agent classifies from the native hub when present, or from inventory when no hub exists. With backup-confirmed one-pass policy, agents may continue through localized visible nodes, top-layer-only hub cleanup, and append-only AGENTS entry flow without repeated approval while preserving existing notes.
integrate.boundary: Default writes stay under .nexubrain/integration/ unless backup-confirmed one-pass policy is selected. Existing folders, note contents, lower-level double links, and existing AGENTS.md rules must be preserved. With backup confirmation, routine additive visible nodes, top-layer-only hub cleanup, and append-only AGENTS entry flow may proceed under the single setup approval. Destructive migration still requires separate human approval.

interview.description: Write-free configuration interview command. Prints the required questions an agent must ask before start or integrate.

language_policy.note: NexuBrain records the preferred configuration language but does not maintain separate built-in translations for every language.

release_layout.dev_compatibility_note: Older development checkouts may temporarily retain legacy visible files, docs/, and a scripts/ wrapper. The cleaned release surface is .nexubrain/ plus README.md.

knowledge_layer.generated_start_path: <target>/<localized Knowledge directory, e.g. 知识/ for zh-cn>
knowledge_layer.generated_integrate_path: <target>/<localized Knowledge path, e.g. 知识/知识.md for zh-cn> after approval or backup-confirmed one-pass setup, with proposal staged at <target>/.nexubrain/integration/Native-Top-Layer-Proposal.md
knowledge_layer.role: Long-term semantic memory for durable concepts, facts, theories, topic notes, and domain understanding. Knowledge should not be promoted to Tasks unless there is a concrete action.

approved_native_top_layer: <target>/<localized State path, e.g. 状态/状态.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Task path, e.g. 任务/任务.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Knowledge path, e.g. 知识/知识.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Workspace path, e.g. 工作区/工作区.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Evidence Chain path, e.g. 证据链/证据链.md for zh-cn>
<target>/<localized Resource path, e.g. 资源/资源.md for zh-cn>
forbidden_defaults: moving native folders into generated categories
keeping classified native folders or notes as first-level hub entries after cleanup
integration_policy.agent_role: The active agent must inspect the existing hub and double-link structure when present, otherwise start from top-level and Markdown inventories; classify existing notes into native NexuBrain top-layer roles; write hidden review/proposal files first; and, when backup-confirmed one-pass policy is selected, proceed through localized visible nodes, top-layer-only hub cleanup, and append-only AGENTS entry flow without repeated approval.

interview_gate.default_integrate_hub_edit_policy: one-pass-after-backup when backup is confirmed; otherwise proposal-only
interview_gate.agent_rule: When the user asks to configure NexuBrain, the agent must first state that configuration requires a short interview, ask language, target, mode, and backup status, run the write-free guide, and then use one-pass integrate if backup is confirmed or proposal-first integrate if backup is not confirmed.

agents_integration_policy.policy: Existing AGENTS.md rules remain authoritative. NexuBrain first generates .nexubrain/integration/AGENTS-Patch-Proposal.md. With backup-confirmed one-pass integrate, the active agent may append the entry flow during the same setup pass; otherwise it remains proposal-only until approved.

display_alias_policy.canonical_source_paths: Keep stable English template paths inside the hidden .nexubrain framework source.
display_alias_policy.generated_visible_paths: Localize generated visible filenames and wiki-link targets according to the confirmed language; do not rely on aliases alone in Obsidian graph view.
display_alias_policy.template_visibility: Do not copy _Template-* files into the visible graph during normal start or integrate setup.

hub_cleanup_policy.first_level_routes: After classification, the native hub must route first-level NexuBrain navigation only through top-layer categories and approved agent entry points. Backup-confirmed one-pass policy authorizes this routine cleanup without repeated approval.
hub_cleanup_policy.classified_native_links: Once native folders or notes are classified, preserve them through category nodes and remove/demount them from first-level hub mounts. If the hub still directly links classified native material, setup is incomplete.
hub_cleanup_policy.uncertain_links: Keep uncertain links in Agent-Review-Worksheet.md or an approved temporary review section until classified.

hubless_integration_policy.default_behavior: A missing hub does not block integrate mode.
hubless_integration_policy.classification_start: Use Existing Top-Level Items and Existing Markdown Files from Integration-Report.md as the initial map.
hubless_integration_policy.native_hub_creation: Create a native hub after classification when backup-confirmed one-pass policy is selected; otherwise propose a native hub path and language for approval.
hubless_integration_policy.automation_boundary: The installer prepares inventories and proposal surfaces; semantic classification is performed by the active agent.

nexus_policy.integrate_behavior: Respect existing native hub names such as 中枢.md, Nexus.md, Hub.md, Home.md, or Index.md; do not force rename them to Nexus.md.

backup_gate_policy.meaning: The user confirmed that the target is backed up, disposable, or a copied test vault. Routine additive integrate setup can proceed without repeated approval.
not_allowed: removing or rewriting existing AGENTS.md rules

source_readme_visibility_policy.github_release: Keep root README.md visible in the published repository so GitHub and Finder users have an entry point.
source_readme_visibility_policy.local_post_setup: After start or integrate setup, hide the local NexuBrain source README.md as .README.md so Obsidian does not treat it as a graph node.
source_readme_visibility_policy.scope: This hide step affects only the NexuBrain tool/source folder. It must not hide, delete, move, or rewrite user-owned knowledge files.
